1→# Founder OS Memory Engine — Design Spec
     2→
     3→**Date**: 2026-03-11
     4→**Status**: Approved
     5→**Approach**: Infrastructure Layer + Thin Command Plugin (Approach 3)
     6→
     7→## Problem
     8→
     9→The 30 Founder OS plugins operate statelessly — each execution starts fresh with no knowledge of past runs, user preferences, or cross-plugin context. A founder who triages emails daily must re-establish context every time. Knowledge discovered by one plugin (e.g., P21 CRM Sync learning a new contact) is invisible to others (e.g., P01 Inbox Zero).
    10→
    11→## Goals
    12→
    13→1. **Cross-plugin shared memory** — Knowledge discovered by any plugin is available to all others
    14→2. **Adaptive behavior** — System detects usage patterns and auto-adjusts after 3+ confirmations, notifying the user
    15→3. **Context injection** — Before any plugin runs, relevant memories are automatically injected into its context
    16→4. **User control** — User can view, teach, forget, and manage memories via commands and Notion
    17→
    18→## Architecture
    19→
    20→### Two Components
    21→
    22→**1. Infrastructure Layer** (`_infrastructure/memory/`)
    23→Shared skills consumed by all 30 plugins. Manages the local SQLite+HNSW store, context injection, and pattern detection. Not a plugin — no manifest, no commands.
    24→
    25→**2. Memory Hub Plugin** (`founder-os-memory-hub/`)
    26→Thin user-facing plugin with 4 slash commands for memory management, plus Notion sync. Follows the standard Anthropic plugin format.
    27→
    28→### Directory Structure
    29→
    30→```
    31→_infrastructure/memory/
    32→├── SKILL.md                    # Core memory API: store, retrieve, query, delete
    33→├── context-injection/
    34→│   └── SKILL.md                # Auto-inject top 5 relevant memories before plugin execution
    35→├── pattern-detection/
    36→│   └── SKILL.md                # Detect patterns, manage confidence, trigger adaptations
    37→└── schema/
    38→    └── memory-store.sql        # SQLite + HNSW schema
    39→
    40→founder-os-memory-hub/
    41→├── .claude-plugin/
    42→│   └── plugin.json
    43→├── commands/
    44→│   ├── memory-show.md          # /memory:show [company|plugin|all] — view memories
    45→│   ├── memory-forget.md        # /memory:forget <key> — delete a memory
    46→│   ├── memory-teach.md         # /memory:teach "always do X" — explicit memory
    47→│   └── memory-sync.md          # /memory:sync — force local ↔ Notion sync
    48→├── skills/
    49→│   └── notion-sync/
    50→│       └── SKILL.md            # Bidirectional sync: local SQLite ↔ [FOS] Memory DB
    51→├── INSTALL.md
    52→├── QUICKSTART.md
    53→└── README.md
    54→```
    55→
    56→## Data Model
    57→
    58→### Local Store (SQLite + HNSW)
    59→
    60→```sql
    61→-- Core memory entries
    62→CREATE TABLE memories (
    63→  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    64→  key TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,          -- human-readable identifier
    65→  category TEXT NOT NULL,            -- preference | pattern | fact | contact | workflow
    66→  content TEXT NOT NULL,             -- the actual memory content
    67→  source_plugin TEXT,                -- which plugin created this (P01, P21, user, etc.)
    68→  confidence INTEGER DEFAULT 0,     -- 0-100, rises with repeated confirmation
    69→  status TEXT DEFAULT 'candidate',   -- candidate | confirmed | applied | dismissed
    70→  company_id TEXT,                   -- optional: related company page_id
    71→  tags TEXT,                         -- JSON array of tags for filtering
    72→  embedding TEXT,                    -- vector embedding for semantic search
    73→  times_used INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    74→  times_confirmed INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    75→  created_at INTEGER NOT NULL,
    76→  updated_at INTEGER NOT NULL,
    77→  last_used_at INTEGER,
    78→  expires_at INTEGER                 -- optional TTL for temporal patterns
    79→);
    80→
    81→-- Pattern observations (raw data before becoming memories)
    82→CREATE TABLE observations (
    83→  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    84→  plugin TEXT NOT NULL,
    85→  action TEXT NOT NULL,              -- what the user did
    86→  context TEXT,                      -- surrounding context (JSON)
    87→  observed_at INTEGER NOT NULL
    88→);
    89→
    90→-- Adaptation log (what the system changed and when)
    91→CREATE TABLE adaptations (
    92→  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    93→  memory_id TEXT REFERENCES memories(id),
    94→  plugin TEXT NOT NULL,
    95→  description TEXT NOT NULL,         -- "Now auto-archiving newsletters from tech-daily.com"
    96→  applied_at INTEGER NOT NULL,
    97→  reverted_at INTEGER                -- null if still active
    98→);
    99→```
   100→
   101→### HNSW Vector Index
   102→
   103→- Indexes `memories.embedding` for semantic similarity search
   104→- Used by context injection to find relevant memories for current plugin input
   105→- Local-only, rebuilt from SQLite data on corruption
   106→
   107→### Notion DB: [FOS] Memory
   108→
   109→Added to HQ template. Properties:
   110→
   111→| Property | Type | Description |
   112→|----------|------|-------------|
   113→| Key | title | Human-readable memory identifier |
   114→| Category | select | preference, pattern, fact, contact, workflow |
   115→| Content | rich_text | The memory content |
   116→| Source Plugin | select | P01-P30 or "User" |
   117→| Confidence | number (0-100) | How certain the system is |
   118→| Status | select | candidate, confirmed, applied, dismissed |
   119→| Company | relation → Companies | Optional company link |
   120→| Tags | multi_select | Filtering tags |
   121→| Times Used | number | Usage count |
   122→| Last Used | date | Last access timestamp |
   123→| Synced At | date | Last local↔Notion sync time |
   124→
   125→## Data Flow
   126→
   127→### 1. Context Injection (before every plugin run)
   128→
   129→```
   130→Plugin starts → context-injection skill activates
   131→  → Extract key entities from input (company names, email domains, topics)
   132→  → Query HNSW index for top 5 semantically similar memories
   133→  → Also query by exact company_id if a company is identified
   134→  → Filter: only status=confirmed or status=applied memories
   135→  → Inject as a "Memory Context" block at the top of the plugin's execution context
   136→  → Increment times_used on each injected memory
   137→```
   138→
   139→Injected format:
   140→```
   141→## Relevant Memories
   142→- [preference] Acme Corp: VIP client, always prioritize. Prefers formal tone. (confidence: 95)
   143→- [contact] Sarah Chen (sarah@acmecorp.com): CTO, key decision maker, has open $45K deal. (confidence: 88)
   144→- [pattern] Emails from @acmecorp.com are typically action-required (85% of last 20). (confidence: 78)
   145→```
   146→
   147→### 2. Pattern Detection (after every plugin run)
   148→
   149→```
   150→Plugin completes → pattern-detection skill activates
   151→  → Log observation: {plugin, action, context, timestamp}
   152→  → Check for emerging patterns:
   153→    - Same action repeated 3+ times? → Create candidate memory
   154→    - User edited/overrode plugin output? → Log preference signal
   155→    - New entity discovered? → Create fact memory
   156→  → For existing candidate memories:
   157→    - Pattern confirmed again? → Increment confidence (+15 per confirmation)
   158→    - Confidence ≥ 60? → Promote to status=confirmed
   159→    - Confidence ≥ 80 and 3+ confirmations? → Auto-adapt, notify user, set status=applied
   160→  → Decay: memories not used in 30 days lose 5 confidence/week
   161→  → Memories below confidence 10 auto-expire
   162→```
   163→
   164→### 3. Auto-Adaptation + Notification
   165→
   166→When a pattern reaches the adaptation threshold (confidence ≥ 80, confirmed 3+ times):
   167→
   168→1. System applies the adaptation (e.g., adjusts priority, pre-fills field, skips step)
   169→2. Creates an adaptation log entry
   170→3. Notifies user in the plugin's output:
   171→   ```
   172→   Memory adapted: I'm now auto-flagging @acmecorp.com emails as VIP based on your last 5 sessions. Run /memory:show to review, or /memory:forget "acme-vip" to revert.
   173→   ```
   174→4. User can revert anytime via `/memory:forget`
   175→
   176→### 4. Notion Sync
   177→
   178→Bidirectional sync between local SQLite and [FOS] Memory DB:
   179→
   180→- **Local → Notion**: Push confirmed/applied memories on plugin completion (debounced, max 1 sync per 5 minutes)
   181→- **Notion → Local**: Pull on plugin start if last sync > 1 hour ago. User edits in Notion (status changes, deletions) propagate to local store.
   182→- **Conflict resolution**: Notion wins for status/content changes (user is source of truth). Local wins for confidence/usage counters (system-tracked).
   183→- **Force sync**: `/memory:sync` triggers immediate bidirectional sync.
   184→
   185→## Integration with Existing Plugins
   186→
   187→Each of the 30 plugins needs a small modification:
   188→
   189→1. **Add to skill references**: Include `_infrastructure/memory/context-injection/SKILL.md` and `_infrastructure/memory/pattern-detection/SKILL.md` in the command markdown's execution flow
   190→2. **Context injection point**: At the start of execution, after input parsing but before main logic
   191→3. **Pattern detection point**: At the end of execution, after output is generated
   192→
   193→This is a 2-3 line addition per command file — no structural changes to any plugin.
   194→
   195→Example addition to a command file:
   196→```markdown
   197→## Step 0: Memory Context
   198→Read the context-injection skill at `_infrastructure/memory/context-injection/SKILL.md`.
   199→Query for memories relevant to the current input. Inject any returned memories into your working context.
   200→
   201→... (existing plugin logic) ...
   202→
   203→## Final Step: Memory Update
   204→Read the pattern-detection skill at `_infrastructure/memory/pattern-detection/SKILL.md`.
   205→Log this execution as an observation. Check for emerging patterns.
   206→```
   207→
   208→## Memory Categories
   209→
   210→| Category | Examples | Source |
   211→|----------|----------|--------|
   212→| preference | "Prefers bullet-point briefings", "Always CC mike@team.com" | User override detection, /memory:teach |
   213→| pattern | "Emails from @bigclient.com are 90% action-required" | Auto-detected from repeated observations |
   214→| fact | "Sarah Chen is CTO at Acme Corp", "Acme Corp fiscal year ends June" | Discovered by P20/P21/P10, or /memory:teach |
   215→| contact | "mike@greenleaf.com — Account Manager, responsive, prefers Slack" | P21 CRM Sync, P01 email patterns |
   216→| workflow | "After invoice processing, founder always runs expense report" | Sequential plugin usage patterns |
   217→
   218→## Commands
   219→
   220→### /memory:show [filter]
   221→Show memories, optionally filtered by company, plugin, category, or status.
   222→- `/memory:show` — all confirmed+applied memories
   223→- `/memory:show acme` — memories related to Acme Corp
   224→- `/memory:show --candidates` — pending patterns awaiting confirmation
   225→- `/memory:show --adaptations` — active behavior changes
   226→
   227→### /memory:forget <key>
   228→Delete a memory and revert any adaptation it triggered.
   229→- `/memory:forget acme-vip` — removes memory, reverts auto-VIP behavior
   230→
   231→### /memory:teach "<statement>"
   232→Explicitly create a confirmed memory (confidence 100).
   233→- `/memory:teach "Always flag emails from @bigclient.com as urgent"`
   234→- `/memory:teach "Acme Corp fiscal year ends in June"`
   235→
   236→### /memory:sync
   237→Force immediate bidirectional sync between local store and [FOS] Memory Notion DB.
   238→
   239→## Performance Constraints
   240→
   241→- Context injection must add < 2 seconds to plugin startup
   242→- HNSW query for top 5 memories: < 100ms (local index)
   243→- Pattern detection runs async after plugin output is delivered to user
   244→- Notion sync is debounced (max 1 per 5 min) to avoid rate limits
   245→- Local store size cap: 10,000 memories (with auto-expiry for low-confidence entries)
   246→
   247→## Non-Goals
   248→
   249→- No plugin-to-plugin real-time messaging (plugins don't talk to each other directly)
   250→- No ML model training (pattern detection uses rule-based heuristics + confidence scoring)
   251→- No memory sharing across different Founder OS installations (single-user system)
   252→

1→# Founder OS Memory Engine — Implementation Plan
     2→
     3→> **Status: Complete** — all 18 tasks implemented and merged to main. Plan updated 2026-03-13 to reflect Notion CLI migration and single-plugin architecture consolidation.
     4→
     5→> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (if subagents available) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan. Steps use checkbox (`- [x]`) syntax for tracking.
     6→
     7→**Goal:** Add cross-plugin shared memory with adaptive behavior to Founder OS — a local SQLite+HNSW store with context injection, pattern detection, Notion sync, and 4 user-facing commands.
     8→
     9→**Architecture:** Infrastructure layer (`_infrastructure/memory/`) provides 3 shared skills consumed by all namespaces. Commands and skills (`commands/memory/`, `skills/memory/`) provide user-facing commands and Notion sync. Local SQLite+HNSW for fast runtime queries; `[FOS] Memory` Notion DB for visibility and user editing.
    10→
    11→**Tech Stack:** SQLite (WAL mode), HNSW vector index (via claude-flow), Notion CLI (`scripts/notion-tool.mjs`), Anthropic plugin format (markdown commands/skills).
    12→
    13→**Spec:** `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-11-memory-engine-design.md`
    14→
    15→---
    16→
    17→## Chunk 1: Infrastructure — Local Store & Core Memory Skill

    35→- `memories` — core memory entries with key, category, content, confidence, status, company_id, embedding, usage counters, timestamps
    36→- `observations` — raw pattern observations before they become memories

    54→
    55→### Task 2: Write core memory SKILL.md
    56→
    57→**Files:**
    58→- Create: `_infrastructure/memory/SKILL.md`
    59→
    60→- [x] **Step 1: Write the core memory skill**
    61→
    62→This skill defines how any plugin reads/writes/queries the local memory store. It must cover:
    63→
    64→**Overview section:**
    65→- Purpose: shared cross-plugin memory for Founder OS
    66→- Store location: `.memory/memory.db` in project root (distinct from `.swarm/`)
    67→- Auto-initialization: if DB doesn't exist, create it from `_infrastructure/memory/schema/memory-store.sql`
    68→
    69→**Store a Memory section:**
    70→- Function: `memory_store(key, category, content, source_plugin, company_id?, tags?, confidence?)`
    71→- If key already exists: update content, increment times_confirmed, bump confidence +15 (cap 100), update updated_at
    72→- If key is new: insert with default confidence=50 for explicit (user-taught), confidence=20 for auto-detected
    73→- Generate embedding from content for HNSW indexing (use same approach as `.swarm/` — JSON array of floats)
    74→- Validate category is one of: preference, pattern, fact, contact, workflow
    75→
    76→**Retrieve Memories section:**
    77→- `memory_query(text, limit=5)` — semantic search via HNSW, returns top N by cosine similarity, filtered to status IN (confirmed, applied)
    78→- `memory_get(key)` — exact key lookup
    79→- `memory_by_company(company_id, limit=10)` — all memories for a company
    80→- `memory_by_plugin(plugin, limit=10)` — all memories created by a plugin
    81→- All retrievals increment `access_count` and update `last_used_at`
    82→
    83→**Delete a Memory section:**
    84→- `memory_delete(key)` — remove memory and its embedding from HNSW
    85→- Also check `adaptations` table — if this memory has an active adaptation, mark it reverted
    86→
    87→**Confidence & Decay section:**
    88→- Confidence range: 0-100
    89→- Confirmation: +15 per repeated observation (cap 100)
    90→- Decay: memories not used in 30 days lose 5 confidence per week
    91→- Auto-expire: memories below confidence 10 are deleted
    92→- Promotion thresholds: ≥60 → confirmed, ≥80 + 3 confirmations → applied
    93→
    94→**Initialization section:**
    95→- Check if `.memory/memory.db` exists
    96→- If not: `mkdir -p .memory && sqlite3 .memory/memory.db < _infrastructure/memory/schema/memory-store.sql`
    97→- If exists: verify schema version, run migrations if needed
    98→
    99→Follow the same markdown skill format as `_infrastructure/gws-skills/gws-common/SKILL.md` — YAML frontmatter with name and description, then structured sections with code examples.

   116→
   117→This skill is referenced by every plugin command at the START of execution. It automatically enriches the plugin's context with relevant memories.
   118→
   119→**Overview section:**
   120→- Purpose: inject relevant memories before plugin logic runs
   121→- Trigger: called as "Step 0" in every plugin command
   122→- Performance target: < 2 seconds added to plugin startup
   123→
   124→**Execution Flow section:**
   125→
   126→1. **Initialize store**: Read core memory skill at `_infrastructure/memory/SKILL.md`. Ensure `.memory/memory.db` exists.
   127→2. **Extract entities from input**: Parse the current plugin's input for:
   128→   - Company names or domains (e.g., "Acme Corp", "@acmecorp.com")
   129→   - Contact names or emails
   130→   - Topics or keywords
   131→   - The plugin name itself (for plugin-specific preferences)
   132→3. **Query memories**:
   133→   - Semantic query: `memory_query(input_text_summary, limit=5)` — top 5 by relevance
   134→   - Company query: if a company is identified, `memory_by_company(company_id, limit=3)`
   135→   - Plugin query: `memory_by_plugin(current_plugin, limit=2)` — plugin-specific preferences
   136→   - Deduplicate across all three queries
   137→   - Filter: only `status IN (confirmed, applied)`
   138→   - Cap at 5 total memories (prioritize by confidence DESC, then relevance)
   139→4. **Format injection block**:
   140→   ```
   141→   ## Relevant Memories
   142→   - [category] content (confidence: N)
   143→   - [category] content (confidence: N)
   144→   ...
   145→   ```
   146→5. **Inject**: Add the block to the plugin's working context
   147→6. **Update usage**: Increment `times_used` and `last_used_at` for each injected memory
   148→
   149→**When No Memories Found section:**
   150→- If no relevant memories exist, skip injection silently (no empty block)
   151→- This is the expected state for new installations
   152→
   153→**Graceful Degradation section:**
   154→- If `.memory/memory.db` doesn't exist or is corrupt: skip injection, log warning, continue plugin execution
   155→- Memory injection must NEVER block or fail a plugin's primary function

   166→### Task 4: Write pattern detection skill
   167→
   168→**Files:**
   169→- Create: `_infrastructure/memory/pattern-detection/SKILL.md`
   170→
   171→- [x] **Step 1: Write the pattern detection skill**
   172→
   173→This skill is referenced by every plugin command at the END of execution. It observes what happened and detects emerging patterns.
   174→
   175→**Overview section:**
   176→- Purpose: detect usage patterns and create/promote memories automatically
   177→- Trigger: called as the final step in every plugin command
   178→- Runs after plugin output is delivered to user (non-blocking conceptually)
   179→
   180→**Observation Logging section:**
   181→- After every plugin execution, log an observation:
   182→  ```sql
   183→  INSERT INTO observations (id, plugin, action, context, observed_at)
   184→  VALUES (generated_id, 'P01', 'triage_email', '{"company":"Acme Corp","category":"action","priority":"high"}', timestamp)
   185→  ```
   186→- `action` = the primary action the plugin performed
   187→- `context` = JSON with key details (company, input type, output type, user edits if any)
   188→
   189→**Pattern Detection Rules section:**
   190→
   191→Rule 1 — **Repeated categorization**: If the same plugin categorizes the same entity the same way 3+ times → create candidate memory
   192→- Example: P01 categorizes @acmecorp.com as "action-required" 5 times → memory: "Emails from @acmecorp.com are typically action-required"
   193→
   194→Rule 2 — **User override detection**: If the user edits or overrides a plugin's output → create preference candidate
   195→- Example: User changes briefing format after P02 generates it → memory: "User prefers [detected format] for daily briefings"
   196→- Detection: compare plugin output to final state (if available)
   197→
   198→Rule 3 — **New entity discovery**: If a plugin encounters a new company, contact, or entity not in existing memories → create fact memory
   199→- Example: P21 CRM Sync finds new contact sarah@newclient.com → memory: "Sarah (sarah@newclient.com) — new contact discovered by CRM Sync"
   200→- Initial confidence: 30 (auto-detected fact)
   201→
   202→Rule 4 — **Sequential plugin usage**: If the same sequence of plugins is run 3+ times → create workflow memory
   203→- Example: User runs P01 → P06 → P10 three sessions in a row → memory: "Common workflow: Inbox Triage → Follow-Up Scan → Client Health"
   204→
   205→Rule 5 — **Temporal patterns**: If a plugin is consistently run at the same time of day → create pattern memory
   206→- Example: P02 Daily Briefing run at 8am 5 days in a row → memory: "Daily briefing typically generated at 8:00 AM"
   207→
   208→**Confidence Promotion section:**
   209→
   210→After logging the observation, check existing candidate memories:
   211→1. Does this observation confirm an existing candidate? → increment confidence +15, increment times_confirmed
   212→2. Promotion ladder:
   213→   - confidence ≥ 60 AND status=candidate → promote to status=confirmed
   214→   - confidence ≥ 80 AND times_confirmed ≥ 3 AND status=confirmed → promote to status=applied, trigger adaptation
   215→
   216→**Adaptation Trigger section:**
   217→
   218→When a memory reaches `applied` status:
   219→1. Log the adaptation:
   220→   ```sql
   221→   INSERT INTO adaptations (id, memory_id, plugin, description, applied_at)
   222→   VALUES (generated_id, memory_id, plugin, 'Now auto-flagging @acmecorp.com as VIP', timestamp)
   223→   ```
   224→2. Append notification to plugin output:
   225→   ```
   226→   > Memory adapted: I'm now [description] based on your last [N] sessions.
   227→   > Run /memory:show to review, or /memory:forget "[key]" to revert.
   228→   ```
   229→
   230→**Decay Processing section:**
   231→
   232→Run decay check once per day (on first plugin execution of the day):
   233→1. Find memories where `last_used_at` < 30 days ago
   234→2. Reduce confidence by 5 for each week of inactivity
   235→3. Delete memories where confidence drops below 10
   236→4. For deleted memories with active adaptations: mark adaptation as reverted
   237→
   238→**Graceful Degradation section:**
   239→- If `.memory/memory.db` doesn't exist or is corrupt: skip pattern detection silently
   240→- Pattern detection must NEVER block or fail a plugin's primary function
   241→- If observation logging fails, continue without error
   242→

   247→git commit -m "feat(memory): add pattern detection skill with confidence promotion and adaptation"
   248→```

1→---
     2→description: Force sync between local memory store and Notion [FOS] Memory database
     3→argument-hint: "[--direction push|pull|both] [--dry-run]"
     4→allowed-tools: Read, Bash
     5→execution-mode: background
     6→result-format: summary
     7→---
     8→
     9→# /founder-os:memory:sync
    10→
    11→Force a bidirectional (or one-way) sync between the local SQLite memory store and the `[FOS] Memory` Notion database.
    12→
    13→## Parse Arguments
    14→
    15→Extract these flags from `$ARGUMENTS`:
    16→- `--direction` (string, one of: `push`, `pull`, `both`; default: `both`) — which direction to sync
    17→- `--dry-run` (boolean, default: false) — when present, show what would happen without writing any changes
    18→
    19→## Load Skills
    20→
    21→Read both skills before proceeding:
    22→1. `_infrastructure/memory/SKILL.md` — core memory engine API (SQLite operations, status rules, confidence mechanics)
    23→2. `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/skills/memory/notion-sync/SKILL.md` — Notion field mapping, conflict rules, rate-limit handling
    24→
    25→Then initialize the memory store per the core skill's initialization steps:
    26→```bash
    27→if [ ! -f ".memory/memory.db" ]; then
    28→  echo "Initializing memory store..."
    29→  mkdir -p .memory
    30→  sqlite3 .memory/memory.db < _infrastructure/memory/schema/memory-store.sql
    31→fi
    32→```
    33→
    34→## Preflight Check
    35→Read the preflight skill at `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/_infrastructure/preflight/SKILL.md`.
    36→Run the preflight check for the `memory` namespace.
    37→If the check returns `blocked`, stop execution and display the fix instructions.
    38→If the check returns `degraded`, note which optional sources are unavailable and adjust later steps accordingly.
    39→
    40→## Step 1: Discover [FOS] Memory DB
    41→
    42→Use the DB discovery procedure from the notion-sync skill:
    43→1. Search Notion for `[FOS] Memory`
    44→2. If not found, search for `Founder OS HQ - Memory`
    45→3. If neither found, lazy-create using the schema defined in the notion-sync skill
    46→4. Store the database ID for all subsequent API calls
    47→
    48→## Step 2: Push (Local → Notion)
    49→
    50→Skip if `--direction pull`.
    51→
    52→Query the local store for memories that need pushing:
    53→
    54→```sql
    55→-- Records never synced OR updated since last sync
    56→SELECT id, key, category, content, confidence, status,
    57→       source_plugin, company_id, tags, times_used, last_used_at,
    58→       updated_at, notion_page_id, deleted_at
    59→FROM memories
    60→WHERE deleted_at IS NULL
    61→  AND (notion_page_id IS NULL OR updated_at > last_synced_at)
    62→
    63→UNION ALL
    64→
    65→-- Records deleted locally that have a Notion page
    66→SELECT id, key, category, content, confidence, status,
    67→       source_plugin, company_id, tags, times_used, last_used_at,
    68→       updated_at, notion_page_id, deleted_at
    69→FROM memories
    70→WHERE deleted_at IS NOT NULL
    71→  AND notion_page_id IS NOT NULL
    72→  AND (last_synced_at IS NULL OR deleted_at > last_synced_at);
    73→```
    74→
    75→For each record to push:
    76→
    77→**New records** (no `notion_page_id`):
    78→- Create a new Notion page using the field mapping from the notion-sync skill
    79→- Store the returned Notion page ID in `memories.notion_page_id`
    80→
    81→**Updated records** (has `notion_page_id`):
    82→- Update the existing Notion page with current field values
    83→
    84→**Deleted records** (has `deleted_at` + `notion_page_id`):
    85→- Archive the Notion page (set `archived: true`)
    86→
    87→Batch creates in groups of up to 10. On 429 errors, apply exponential backoff per the notion-sync skill's rate-limit rules.
    88→
    89→Track counts: `pushed_new`, `pushed_updated`, `pushed_deleted`.
    90→
    91→If `--dry-run`: list what would be pushed without executing API calls.
    92→
    93→## Step 3: Pull (Notion → Local)
    94→
    95→Skip if `--direction push`.
    96→
    97→Fetch Notion pages modified since `last_sync_timestamp` (read from `.memory/sync-state.json`, or use epoch 0 for first run):
    98→
    99→```
   100→GET /databases/{db_id}/query
   101→filter: { last_edited_time: { after: last_sync_timestamp } }
   102→```
   103→
   104→For each Notion page:
   105→
   106→**User archived/deleted a page**:
   107→- If `archived: true` and local record exists:
   108→  - Set `deleted_at = now()` in local store
   109→  - Revert any applied adaptations linked to this memory (set downstream behavior back to default)
   110→  - Set `status = 'dismissed'`
   111→
   112→**User changed status in Notion**:
   113→- If Notion status differs from local: apply Notion's status (Notion wins for status)
   114→- If new status is `dismissed`: freeze confidence, disable decay
   115→
   116→**User changed content in Notion**:
   117→- If Notion content differs from local: apply Notion's content (Notion wins for content)
   118→
   119→**Conflict resolution** (applied per field):
   120→- Notion wins: `status`, `content`, `category`, `tags`
   121→- Local wins: `confidence`, `times_used`, `last_used_at`
   122→- Most recent wins: `updated_at` (take whichever timestamp is newer for all other fields not listed above)
   123→
   124→Track counts: `pulled_updated`, `pulled_dismissed`.
   125→
   126→If `--dry-run`: list what would be pulled without writing to local store.
   127→
   128→## Step 4: Update Sync Timestamps
   129→
   130→If not `--dry-run`:
   131→
   132→For all successfully processed memories, update:
   133→```sql
   134→UPDATE memories
   135→SET last_synced_at = datetime('now')
   136→WHERE id IN (:processed_ids);
   137→```
   138→
   139→Write the new sync timestamp to `.memory/sync-state.json`:
   140→```json
   141→{
   142→  "last_sync": "2026-03-11T09:15:00Z",
   143→  "direction": "both"
   144→}
   145→```
   146→
   147→## Step 5: Report Results
   148→
   149→Output the sync summary in this exact format:
   150→
   151→```
   152→Sync complete:
   153→- Pushed: {pushed_new} new, {pushed_updated} updated, {pushed_deleted} deleted
   154→- Pulled: {pulled_updated} updated, {pulled_dismissed} dismissed by user
   155→- Last sync: {timestamp}
   156→```
   157→
   158→If `--dry-run`, prefix the output with:
   159→```
   160→[DRY RUN] No changes written. Would have:
   161→```
   162→
   163→If any errors occurred (network failures, Notion API errors after retries), append:
   164→```
   165→- Errors: {count} — see details below
   166→  - {key}: {error message}
   167→```
   168→
   169→## Error Handling
   170→
   171→- If Notion CLI is not configured or unreachable: halt with message `"Notion CLI unavailable — cannot sync. Check .mcp.json configuration."`
   172→- If local memory store does not exist and cannot be initialized: halt with message `"Memory store not found. Run /founder-os:memory:store first to initialize."`
   173→- If a single record fails to push/pull: log the error and continue with remaining records (never abort the full sync for one bad record)
   174→- After 3 retries with exponential backoff on a record: skip it, add to error count
   175→
   176→## Usage Examples
   177→
   178→```
   179→/founder-os:memory:sync
   180→/founder-os:memory:sync --direction push
   181→/founder-os:memory:sync --direction pull
   182→/founder-os:memory:sync --direction both --dry-run
   183→/founder-os:memory:sync --dry-run
   184→```
   185→

     5→
     6→# Memory Notion Sync
     7→
     8→This skill manages the full lifecycle of syncing the local `.memory/memory.db` SQLite store with the `[FOS] Memory` Notion database. It covers DB discovery, schema, field mapping, conflict resolution, sync triggers, and rate-limit handling.
     9→
    10→---
    11→
    12→## DB Discovery
    13→
    14→Always search in this order to locate the target database. Never hard-code a database ID.
    15→
    16→1. Search Notion for `[FOS] Memory` (exact name match preferred)
    17→2. If not found, search for `Founder OS HQ - Memory`
    18→3. If neither found: lazy-create the database using the schema below (only for non-HQ users who did not install the full HQ template)
    19→
    20→To search via Notion CLI:
    21→```
    22→node ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/scripts/notion-tool.mjs search "[FOS] Memory" --filter database
    23→```
    24→
    25→Store the resolved database ID in memory for the duration of the sync session. Do not re-query on every API call.
    26→
    27→---
    28→
    29→## DB Schema
    30→
    31→The `[FOS] Memory` Notion database has these properties:
    32→
    33→| Property | Notion Type | Notes |
    34→|----------|------------|-------|
    35→| Key | title | Unique identifier for the memory (e.g., `pref:email:archive_newsletters`) |
    36→| Category | select | Options: `preference`, `pattern`, `fact`, `contact`, `workflow` |
    37→| Content | rich_text | The human-readable memory content |
    38→| Source Plugin | select | Options: `P01`–`P30`, `User`, `System` |
    39→| Confidence | number | Integer 0–100 |
    40→| Status | select | Options: `candidate`, `confirmed`, `applied`, `dismissed` |
    41→| Company | relation | Relates to `[FOS] Companies` database (optional) |
    42→| Tags | multi_select | Free-form tags for grouping and filtering |
    43→| Times Used | number | How many times this memory has influenced plugin output |
    44→| Last Used | date | Date of most recent application |
    45→| Synced At | date | Timestamp of most recent successful sync |
    46→
